Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 223,247.35 moved across the accounts in this case.
  • The largest source of funds was NIKHIL, which sent out Rs 66,398.00.
  • ANJALI SUNITA spread money to 45 different accounts.
  • ANJALI SUNITA collected money from 12 different accounts.
  • Rs 3,254,110.00 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals and Rs 40,003.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account to investigate first is AMIT CHAUHAN, which shows the strongest combination of suspicious behaviours in this case.

acct_001 · RBL Bank · Account Number 216655101347 · Score 1,918
Rank #1

This account is suspicious due to multiple high-value transactions and shared UPI identifiers across different accounts, indicating potential coordinated activity or shared ownership. The account exhibits strong patterns and distinct patterns, with a high score of 1918. The money behaviour looks like a complex network of transactions, which raises concerns about the legitimacy of the account activity.

  • Cross Statement Links strong — A debit of 10000.00 from MR. CHHAYA IYER (216655101347) matched a credit of 10000.00 to AMIT CHAUHAN (216655101347) on 2025-05-06, suggesting a suspicious transaction. (+3 more of this type)

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
